module_title: Stressors Affecting Alterations Across the Lifespan
learning_objectives: Identify mental health disorders in children and adolescents.
Recognize signs of eating disorders and appropriate nursing interventions.
Differentiate delirium from dementia in older adults.
Implement nursing strategies tailored to developmental stages.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Mental health needs change from childhood through old age. Nurses adapt assessments and interventions to each developmental stage.

sections.title: Childhood Disorders
sections.content: Neurodevelopmental issues like autism and ADHD often emerge early. Early intervention and family support improve outcomes.

sections.title: Adolescent Concerns
sections.content: Teens may face eating disorders, substance use, and mood changes. Building rapport and involving families are key.

sections.title: Adult Stressors
sections.content: Adults experience work pressures, relationship issues, and major life events. Stress management and counseling can prevent deterioration.

sections.title: Elderly Mental Health
sections.content: Dementia, depression, and delirium are common later-life concerns. Assess cognition and medication effects carefully.

sections.title: Medication Across the Lifespan
sections.content: Dosages and side effects vary with age. Monitor children for growth impacts and older adults for polypharmacy risks.

sections.title: Family and Caregiver Roles
sections.content: Education and respite services support caregivers and reduce burnout at every stage of life.

sections.title: Communication Strategies
sections.content: Use age‑appropriate language and involve caregivers when teaching or providing discharge instructions.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.content: Developmental awareness helps nurses tailor mental health care to meet unique needs across the lifespan.
